summary: Frontend-only verification of Iter-94 HH:MM auto-format on punch inputs. Login + firm inject OK. Manual Entries tab (empty inputs) WORKS perfectly (930→09:30, 1745→17:45, 999→09:09 clamped). BUG REPRODUCED on Auto-Punches tab where inputs are PREFILLED: any typing into a full HH:MM value is silently dropped because formatHHMM strips the colon then does slice(0,4), which throws away newly-typed digits when the string already has 4 numeric chars. This exactly matches the user complaint 'not working' — user is trying to edit auto-punch times by typing over them, but characters vanish. Same latent bug exists in /manual-punch-entry.tsx modal Time input (only matters after user has typed 4 digits and tries to fix a char). Also modal Time TextInput missing testID.

ui_bugs.issue: slice(0,4) discards newly-typed digit when field already holds 4 numeric chars (HH:MM). On Auto-Punches tab prefilled '07:57', typing '8' at end → onChangeText receives '07:578' → digits='07578' → slice(0,4)='0757' → value stays '07:57'. Repro: TEST2b appended '800' to '20:00' → '20:00' (unchanged). TEST2c click+type '9' on '08:00' → '08:09' then typing '30' → still '08:09' (further digits dropped). USER-VISIBLE: cannot edit any prefilled time by typing; must Ctrl+A / clear first.

Input '999' clamps to '09:09' (last '9' dropped) instead of '09:59'. Cause: after prepending 0 → '0999', d[2]='9'>5 triggers `${d.slice(0,2)}0${d[2]}` = '09'+'0'+'9' = '0909' — the original d[3]='9' is lost. Minor UX quirk; MM=09 is confusing.

No testID attribute. Same formatHHMM slice(0,4) bug applies once field has 4 digits — user cannot edit already-typed value. maxLength={5} additionally blocks typing beyond HH:MM which prevents the format function from receiving new digits at all.

integration_issues.issue: Editing existing auto-punch time by tap+type is fundamentally broken; user must select-all then type. This is the reported 'not working' scenario.

action_items: Fix formatHHMM in /app/frontend/app/punch-approvals.tsx (line 281) and /app/frontend/app/manual-punch-entry.tsx (line 87) to handle appended digits when field is already full. Recommended: use `slice(-4)` instead of `slice(0,4)` OR detect (raw.length > previous_value.length && digits.length > 4) → keep last 4 digits. Alternative: on input focus, auto-select-all (onFocus: e => e.target.select() via ref/setNativeProps) so a bare tap-type replaces the value.
Fix '999' clamp: preserve the trailing digit, e.g. reorder clamp so MM overflow becomes 59 not 09. Current line 285 loses d[3].
Add testID='mpe-modal-time' (or similar) to the Time TextInput in manual-punch-entry.tsx line 495 to enable automated testing.
Reconsider maxLength={5} on manual-punch-entry modal Time input — combined with the slice(0,4) bug it blocks any edit after HH:MM is complete.
Consider adding a small 'Clear' (×) button inside each prefilled time input on Auto-Punches so user has a discoverable way to replace the value.
critical_code_review_comments: formatHHMM slice(0,4) is fine for building a value from scratch but wrong for a controlled input receiving user edits — it treats every keystroke as if starting from raw digits. This is the classic RN-web pitfall with controlled TextInputs: onChangeText fires with the whole DOM value including inserted char, not a delta.
punch-approvals.tsx is 1316 lines — recommended to split (input rows, save flow, filters) into smaller modules.
Manual Punch Entry modal Time TextInput lacks testID; harder to write regression tests for the exact scenario that was requested.

success_rate.frontend: 60% (Manual Entries OK, Auto-Punches editing BROKEN, MPE modal not testID-instrumented)

seed_data_creation: None; used existing 2026-07-01 (Auto complete punches) and 2026-07-02 (Manual missing) real biometric data. No writes performed (Save flow not exercised because the reproduce bug happens before Save enables).
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes formatHHMM for the append-to-full-field case, re-run: (1) Auto-Punches TEST2b — append '800' to '20:00' at end → expected '20:00' unchanged is WRONG; new behavior should replace to '08:00' or insert with slice(-4). (2) Manual Entries TEST1c '999' clamp — verify improved clamp. (3) Save regression: after edit, page.on('dialog', d=>d.accept('agent test')), click ds-save-<key>, verify success alert + row updated + cleanup DELETE via /api/admin/attendance/{id}?reason=cleanup with bearer from POST /api/auth/admin-password-login {email:'sksharma',password:'sharma123'}. TestIDs on punch-approvals: pa-date-input, tab-manual, tab-auto, pa-show, ds-in-<key>, ds-out-<key>, ds-save-<key>. Firm inject: localStorage.setItem('skc:selected_company','cmp_527fecdd7c') AFTER login on /admin-pin-login (identifier=sksharma, password=sharma123).
rca_of_issue:
ROOT CAUSE: formatHHMM(raw) at /app/frontend/app/punch-approvals.tsx:281 and /app/frontend/app/manual-punch-entry.tsx:87 starts with `let d = raw.replace(/[^0-9]/g, '').slice(0, 4);`. On React-Native-Web, the controlled TextInput fires onChangeText with the ENTIRE new DOM value (including the character inserted at the caret). When the field already contains a valid HH:MM (4 numeric characters), any additional keystroke yields 5 digits after stripping the colon; slice(0,4) keeps the ORIGINAL FIRST FOUR digits and discards the newly-typed one, so the input appears frozen. 

REPRO STEPS (verified by agent 2026-07-09):
1. Login /admin-pin-login as sksharma/sharma123, inject skc:selected_company=cmp_527fecdd7c
2. Go /punch-approvals, set pa-date-input=2026-07-01, click tab-auto, click pa-show
3. Click an input showing '07:57', type '8' at end → value STAYS '07:57' (should be '08:00' or similar)
4. Try another '20:00' input, .type('800') without clearing → value STAYS '20:00'
5. Click '08:00' input, type '9' → '08:09' (cursor was mid-value), continue typing '30' → still '08:09' (further digits swallowed)

MITIGATION (main agent to implement):
A) Change `.slice(0, 4)` to `.slice(-4)` so newest digits win. Test cases: '07578' → '7578' → '75:78' → but wait that fails hour clamp. Better: keep slice(0,4) but if raw contains a colon AND raw digit-count > 4, use slice(-4). Pseudo:
```
const allDigits = raw.replace(/[^0-9]/g,'');
let d = allDigits.length > 4 && /:/.test(raw) ? allDigits.slice(-4) : allDigits.slice(0,4);
```
B) OR simpler: on TextInput focus, select-all (web: onFocus={e => e.target?.select?.()}), so a plain tap+type always replaces.
C) Additionally on manual-punch-entry.tsx: remove maxLength={5} OR set to 6 to allow the transitional 5-digit state to reach onChangeText.
